page.title: Málaga Fair Yacht Charter in Marbella: A Fiesta from the Sea

data.meta_description: Book a Málaga Fair yacht charter in Marbella. Skip the traffic, arrive by sea. Prices from €749/2h. Fleet departs Puerto Banús. Reserve your boat now.
data.summary: A Málaga Fair yacht charter from Marbella lets you cruise from Puerto Banús to the fair in style, avoiding road traffic. Our fleet includes boats from €749/2h, all with skipper, drinks, and VAT included.
key_facts: Málaga Fair yacht charter departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella.
Prices from €749 for 2 hours on Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (all incl.).
Mangusta 80 charter from €4,719 for 4 hours (jet ski included).
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT.
Cruise time to Málaga port: approx. 1.5–2 hours (25 NM).
Fleet capacity: 5 to 12 passengers per boat.
Book via WhatsApp for custom durations and boat options.

faq.q: What is the best boat for a Málaga Fair yacht charter from Marbella?
faq.a: For a group up to 9, the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) or Azimut 39 (€749/2h) are excellent value. If you want speed and luxury, the Mangusta 80 (from €4,719/4h) includes a jet ski. All depart Puerto Banús.

faq.q: How long does it take to sail from Puerto Banús to Málaga?
faq.a: The distance is about 25 nautical miles. Depending on boat speed, expect 1.5 to 2 hours. A faster vessel like the Mangusta 80 can do it in around 1 hour.

faq.q: Can we anchor near the Málaga Fair and watch from the sea?
faq.a: Yes, anchoring off Málaga’s coastline is possible, subject to weather and local regulations. Your skipper will choose the best spot. Alternatively, dock at Málaga Marina and walk to the fair.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs.

faq.q: Can I book a charter for just a few hours during the fair?
faq.a: Absolutely. Our Tier A boats start at 2-hour charters. For longer stays, 4- or 8-hour options are available. Contact us via WhatsApp to tailor the timing.

faq.q: Is the Málaga Fair accessible by foot from the marina?
faq.a: Yes, Málaga Marina is a short walk (about 15 minutes) from the fairgrounds. Your skipper can assist with docking and directions.

faq.q: What if the weather is bad on the day of the fair?
faq.a: Your safety is paramount. If conditions are unsafe, we will reschedule or offer a full refund. Our skippers monitor forecasts closely.

faq.q: How do I book a Málaga Fair yacht charter from Marbella?
faq.a: Call or WhatsApp us directly. Provide your preferred date, group size, and budget. We’ll confirm availability and send a booking link. Payment secures your slot.

data.body_html:
Málaga Fair (Feria de Málaga) is one of Andalucía’s biggest summer parties—two weeks of flamenco, casetas, and carnival rides. But getting there from Marbella by road? Gridlock on the A-7. A smarter move: book a Málaga Fair yacht charter Marbella and arrive by sea. Depart from Puerto Banús, cruise the Costa del Sol, dock at Málaga Marina, and step straight into the fiesta. No traffic, no parking stress—just sun, sea, and sangria.

Why Choose a Yacht Charter for Málaga Fair?

The Málaga Fair draws over a million visitors. Roads into the city become parking lots. A yacht charter flips the script: you sail from Marbella in comfort, anchor off the coast or dock at Málaga Marina, and enjoy the fair on your own schedule. Plus, the journey itself is a highlight—views of La Concha, the Sierra Blanca, and the Mediterranean coastline.

Our Fleet for Málaga Fair Charters

All our boats depart from Puerto Banús. Here are the top picks for the fair:

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) – €749/2h, €2,299/8h. Ideal for small groups.
  • Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – same pricing. Slightly more capacity.
  • Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) – from €4,719/4h, includes a jet ski. Fast and flashy.
  • Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) – pricing on request. Spacious flybridge.
  • Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) – flagship luxury. Contact us for rates.

For full fleet details, see our boat rental Marbella page.

Pricing & Inclusions

Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours—all inclusive. The Mangusta 80 starts at €4,719 for 4 hours (jet ski included). Other boats: pricing on request via WhatsApp.

Every charter includes: licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and 21% Spanish VAT. No surprises.

The Route: Puerto Banús to Málaga

The distance is roughly 25 nautical miles. A typical cruise takes 1.5–2 hours, depending on boat speed. You’ll pass Marbella’s Golden Mile, Cabopino, Cala del Faro, and Cala Cortés before rounding the point toward Málaga. Your skipper can anchor off the beach near the fair or dock at Málaga Marina (a 15-minute walk to the fairgrounds).

What to Expect at Málaga Fair

The fair runs for two weeks in August (dates vary yearly). Highlights include traditional casetas (tented bars with live music), flamenco shows, fairground rides, and food stalls serving espetos (sardines on sticks) and local wine. It’s family-friendly, loud, and unforgettable.

Book Your Málaga Fair Yacht Charter

Ready to skip the traffic? Contact us on WhatsApp with your preferred date, group size, and budget. We’ll recommend the best boat and confirm availability. Slots fill quickly during fair season—book early. For more ideas, check our Málaga Fair yacht charter Marbella guide.

Frequently asked questions

What is the best boat for a Málaga Fair yacht charter from Marbella?

For a group up to 9, the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) or Azimut 39 (€749/2h) are excellent value. If you want speed and luxury, the Mangusta 80 (from €4,719/4h) includes a jet ski. All depart Puerto Banús.

How long does it take to sail from Puerto Banús to Málaga?

The distance is about 25 nautical miles. Depending on boat speed, expect 1.5 to 2 hours. A faster vessel like the Mangusta 80 can do it in around 1 hour.

Can we anchor near the Málaga Fair and watch from the sea?

Yes, anchoring off Málaga’s coastline is possible, subject to weather and local regulations. Your skipper will choose the best spot. Alternatively, dock at Málaga Marina and walk to the fair.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs.

Can I book a charter for just a few hours during the fair?

Absolutely. Our Tier A boats start at 2-hour charters. For longer stays, 4- or 8-hour options are available. Contact us via WhatsApp to tailor the timing.

Is the Málaga Fair accessible by foot from the marina?

Yes, Málaga Marina is a short walk (about 15 minutes) from the fairgrounds. Your skipper can assist with docking and directions.

What if the weather is bad on the day of the fair?

Your safety is paramount. If conditions are unsafe, we will reschedule or offer a full refund. Our skippers monitor forecasts closely.

How do I book a Málaga Fair yacht charter from Marbella?

Call or WhatsApp us directly. Provide your preferred date, group size, and budget. We’ll confirm availability and send a booking link. Payment secures your slot.
